task_description: Change the default assistant model in settings to a different option and return to the main home screen.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the left vertical sidebar, with a semitransparent red X centered on a gear-shaped settings icon inside a light gray square button. A small dark tooltip beside it reads “设置”. In the full-screen view, the Poe website is open in a browser, with the same left navigation rail visible and the main page showing the Poe logo, model tabs, and a chat input area in the center.
caption.action: Click the gear-shaped settings button in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The settings area should open, changing the main view or showing the app's settings page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a white dropdown field under the label for default app, with a semitransparent red X centered on the currently selected option text “Gemini-3.1-Pro.” This is a UI control in the settings form. In the full-screen view, the Poe settings page is open in Chrome on the 通用 tab, with sections for email, phone number, default app, appearance, and language visible; the same dropdown sits in the middle of the page.
caption.action: Click the default app dropdown showing “Gemini-3.1-Pro.”
caption.expectation: The dropdown menu should open, showing available app or model options so one can be selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the entire screen with no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. A centered white modal titled “选择默认机器人” is open over a dimmed settings page. Inside the modal is a scrollable list of bot options, currently showing entries including “Assistant,” “Script-Bot-Creator,” “App-Creator,” “Claude-Opus-4.6,” and “Nano-Banana-2,” each with a distinctive icon on the left and descriptive Chinese text. A thin scrollbar is visible along the right side of the modal’s content area, indicating more items below. In the full-screen view, this modal sits over the Poe settings page in Chrome on the 通用 tab, with the left navigation rail and the dimmed settings form visible behind it.
caption.action: Scroll down within the bot-selection list in the centered modal to view more available default robot options below.
caption.expectation: The list inside the modal should move downward, revealing additional bot choices farther down the available options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the description text area inside the top list item, with a semitransparent red X centered over the Chinese description beneath the title “GPT-5.4-Mini.” The item has the multicolored OpenAI knot icon on the left, the bold model name on the right, smaller gray descriptive text below, and a small “官方” label near the bottom of the card. This is an object within the modal list rather than a toolbar control. In the full-screen view, a centered white modal for choosing the default bot is open over the dimmed Poe settings page, and the top visible option “GPT-5.4-Mini” is outlined, above entries such as “Gemini-3.1-Pro,” “GPT-5.4-Pro,” “Gemini-3.1-Flash-Lite,” and “GPT-5.3-Codex.”
caption.action: Click the top model option “GPT-5.4-Mini” in the selection list, specifically the descriptive text area under its title.
caption.expectation: That model option should become selected as the default choice, likely closing the modal or otherwise confirming the selection in the settings page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the top-left navigation rail of the Poe page. A semitransparent red X is centered on the Poe robot-head logo button inside the vertical sidebar, above other sidebar icons such as a speech bubble and additional navigation symbols. This target is a UI control in the app’s left toolbar area. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to Poe settings on the 通用 tab, with the default app now showing “GPT-5.4-Mini,” and the left sidebar remains visible along the page edge.
caption.action: Click the Poe robot-head logo button at the top of the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The app should leave the settings view and navigate to Poe’s main or home area.
